claims.claim: BioProver verifies all 24 benchmark circuits (14 at Sound, 10 at Bounded level)
BioProver is 2–5× faster than dReach on circuits both tools can handle
BioProver is the only tool to produce a result on circuits with ≥10 species within a 300-second timeout
Biology-aware refinement reduces CEGAR iterations by 48%
ML-guided predicate suggestion provides a further 32% reduction in CEGAR iterations
Scales to 15-species circuits via compositional verification (106.7s using 5 modules, where monolithic times out at 300s)
Compositional CEGAR achieves 3.1× speedup at 12 species
Compositional approach shows sub-quadratic O(n^1.5) scaling
Monolithic approach shows approximately exponential scaling with species count
SoundnessLevel hierarchy (Sound > δ-Sound > Bounded > Approximate) tracks formal guarantee levels
SoundnessAnnotation tracks level, assumptions, δ value, time bound, and approximation error
Soundness composition uses meet (weakest level) of component annotations
ValidatedInterval uses mpmath arbitrary-precision interval arithmetic (113-bit precision) for rigorous enclosures
Interval class uses IEEE 754 double-precision with nextafter-based outward rounding
Biology-aware CEGAR with refinement strategies: structural, monotonicity, and AI-guided
CEGAR loop maintains predicate abstraction, encodes as SMT, invokes dReal, validates counterexamples, and refines
Monotonicity refinement exploits sign-definite Jacobian structure and Hill-function thresholds
AI-guided refinement suggests predicates from ML model, accepted only if quality monitor confirms accuracy
Quality-gated ML with auto-disable: PredictionQualityMonitor tracks precision and NDCG@5, disengages ML when quality drops below thresholds (precision < 0.5, NDCG@5 < 0.6)
Quality monitor uses a sliding window of 3 CEGAR iterations
ML component achieves 90.3% precision with 66.8% recall across 24 benchmarks
Average CEGAR speedup from ML guidance is 1.53×
Low false positive rate: 15 out of 432 candidates (3.5%)
Circular AG well-formedness formalized with circular consistency and contraction-based convergence
Fixed-point iteration for circular AG with assumption update and convergence check
Contract-based compositional decomposition along monotone subsystem boundaries
Automatic GRN decomposition into modules for circuits with >5 species
Bio-STL domain macros (adapts, bistable, oscillates, dose_response, reaches_ss, mutex, gate, propagates, amplifies, quorum, damps)
Bio-STL macros expand to standard STL formulas; expansion is purely syntactic and preserves semantics
STL robustness quantitative semantics computed over signals
CEGIS-based parameter repair searches for parameter adjustments that eliminate counterexamples while preserving verified properties
CEGIS alternates synthesis and verification phases with minimum separation constraint for δ-decidability
QR preconditioning for wrapping effect mitigation reduces interval growth from ~1.5 to ~1.05 per iteration
GraphSAGE GNN for circuit topology encoding
GP surrogate with uncertainty quantification for robustness estimation (SurrogateVerdict)
Theorem 1 (Robustness SMT Encoding): STL robustness semantics faithfully encoded as SMT formula over ODE solutions
Theorem 2 (CEGAR Progress): Biology-aware CEGAR with monotonicity refinement terminates in O(k·2^n) iterations
Theorem 3 (CEGIS Convergence): CEGIS loop either finds a valid repair or proves no repair exists within Θ, in finitely many iterations
Theorem 4 (Circular AG Convergence): Fixed-point iteration converges to unique fixed point in O(log(1/ε)/log(1/γ)) iterations when update operator is a contraction
Proposition (Meet Compositionality): Overall soundness is the meet of all component soundness levels
Proposition (Contraction Sufficient Condition): Spectral radius condition on inter/intra-module Jacobians guarantees contraction
Proposition (Enclosure Property): ValidatedInterval operations produce guaranteed enclosures for all elementary functions
dReach baseline comparison: BioProver faster on every benchmark where dReach terminates, average speedup 3.4×
Breach baseline comparison: falsification tool that times out on all circuits with ≥8 species
Largest speedup over dReach is 4.7× on mapk_cascade (53.4s vs 252.4s)
Full system converges in 7–26 CEGAR iterations across all 24 benchmarks
Removing AI guidance increases CEGAR iterations by 57% (toggle switch) and 40% (repressilator)
Structural refinement alone increases iterations by 114% (toggle switch) and 80% (repressilator)
Compositional approach uses 35–40% less memory than monolithic
Per-circuit ML quality metrics for all 24 benchmarks with precision >0.75 for 21 of 24 circuits
Leave-one-circuit-out cross-validation methodology for ML evaluation
ML predicate suggestion model is gradient-boosted decision tree (XGBoost)
Feature extraction from counterexample traces (peak values, settling times, oscillation frequencies) and circuit topology (in-degree, out-degree, feedback loop membership)
60% of synthetic biology circuits fail on first physical build (Nielsen et al. 2016)
δ-decidability provides practical foundation for nonlinear ODE verification
CEGAR paradigm originally introduced by Clarke et al.
Circular AG rules formalized by McMillan and Namjoshi & Trefler
Robustness SMT encoding translates Bio-STL to SMT-LIB via recursive descent over macro syntax tree
Hill function encoding for activation and repression
SMT-LIB serialization for solver interface
dReal SMT solver interface for δ-satisfiability checking
Craig interpolation for structural refinement predicates
GRN modeled as ODE system with Hill-function right-hand sides
SBML file import for GRN models
Monotone subsystem identification for decomposition
Counterexample validation by ODE simulation from counterexample initial conditions
Predicate abstraction management in CEGAR loop
Ablation experiment infrastructure for refinement strategy comparison
Scalability experiment infrastructure for monolithic vs compositional comparison
Baseline comparison infrastructure for dReach and Breach
14 circuits with ≤5 species and standard topologies achieve Sound verification
Type-2 feed-forward loops achieve Bounded guarantees due to AND-gate dynamics requiring bounded model checking
Single Unknown result (random_grn_6) has no exploitable monotonicity structure
Biosensor_amp achieves Sound via cascade-like topology with no circular dependencies
Quality gate was never triggered on any of the 24 benchmarks
Proof certificate generation for verification results
Taylor model arithmetic for ODE integration
Flowpipe computation for reachability analysis
Contract synthesis for compositional proof composition
Robustness optimization in parameter repair
Design space exploration for parameter synthesis
